analysis:
## Key Files & Integration Points

### Session Naming & ACP Infrastructure
- `src/agents/acp/adapter.ts:184-202` ã `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName?, storyId?, sessionRole?)` produces `nax-<hash8>-<feature>-<storyId>-<role>`. `ensureAcpSession()` (lines 212-238) resumes an existing named session or creates a new one (fail-open).
- `src/agents/types.ts:53-96` ã `AgentRunOptions` already has `sessionRole?: string`, `keepSessionOpen?: boolean`, `acpSessionName?: string`, `featureName?: string`, `storyId?: string`.

### Execution Stage (US-001 target)
- `src/pipeline/stages/execution.ts:251-275` ã single-session `agent.run()` for no-test/test-after strategies. Currently passes `featureName: ctx.prd.feature` and `storyId: ctx.story.id` but NO `sessionRole` (line 269 comment: 'No sessionRole for single-session strategies'). No `keepSessionOpen`. This produces session name `nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>` (no role suffix), mismatching TDD's `nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-implementer`.

### TDD Session (reference pattern)
- `src/tdd/session-runner.ts:171` ã `keepSessionOpen = role === 'implementer' && (config.execution.rectification?.enabled ?? false)`. Line 191: passes `sessionRole: role`. This is the pattern to replicate.
- `src/tdd/rectification-gate.ts:150-152` ã `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, 'implementer')` ã exact pattern for resuming implementer session.

### Semantic Review (US-002/US-003 targets)
- `src/review/semantic.ts:291-298` ã `runSemanticReview(workdir, storyGitRef, story, semanticConfig, modelResolver, naxConfig)` ã current signature has no `featureName` parameter.
- `src/review/semantic.ts:472-478` ã `agent.complete()` with hardcoded `sessionName: \`nax-semantic-${story.id}\`` ã bypasses `buildSessionName()`. Must be replaced with `agent.run()` targeting the implementer session.
- `src/review/semantic.ts:382-393` ã debate branch creates `DebateSession` with `featureName: story.id` (BUG: story ID used as feature name).

### Review Call Chain
- `src/pipeline/stages/review.ts:47-65` ã calls `reviewOrchestrator.review()` with `ctx.config`, `ctx.story`, etc. Has access to `ctx.prd.feature` but does NOT pass it as a separate `featureName` param.
- `src/review/orchestrator.ts:75-102` ã `ReviewOrchestrator.review()` calls `runReview()` ã no `featureName` parameter threaded through.
- `src/review/runner.ts:273-280` ã `runSemanticReview()` call site ã passes `workdir, storyGitRef, semanticStory, semanticCfg, modelResolver, naxConfig`. No `featureName`.

### Debate Resolution (US-004 target)
- `src/debate/session-helpers.ts:176-235` ã `resolveOutcome(proposalOutputs, critiqueOutputs, stageConfig, config, storyId, timeoutMs)` ã no `workdir` or `featureName` params. Synthesis resolver (line 197-206) and judge resolver (line 217-227) pass `completeOptions` but no `acpSessionName`.
- `src/debate/session-stateful.ts:27-35` ã `StatefulCtx` type has `workdir` and `featureName` fields (already present).
- `src/debate/session-stateful.ts:268-276` ã calls `resolveOutcome()` with `ctx.storyId, ctx.config, ctx.stageConfig` ã does NOT pass `ctx.workdir` or `ctx.featureName`.

### Autofix (reference pattern)
- `src/pipeline/stages/autofix.ts:290-302` ã `agent.run()` with `sessionRole: 'implementer'` ã consistent with TDD. Parallel pattern for US-003.

## Risks & Mitigations
1. **Backward compatibility:** All changes are additive (new optional params). Existing callers without `featureName` produce slightly different session names (missing feature segment) but `ensureAcpSession()` fails open ã no regression.
2. **Session lifetime:** Adding `keepSessionOpen: true` to execution.ts means sessions persist longer. Must ensure the condition is gated on `review.enabled || rectification.enabled` to avoid dangling sessions.
3. **complete() ã run() in semantic review:** `agent.run()` returns `AgentRunResult` vs `agent.complete()` returning `string | CompleteResult`. The response extraction logic must adapt. `run()` result has `.output` field.
4. **Debate resolver uses complete() not run():** The synthesis/custom resolvers call `adapter.complete()` via `synthesisResolver()`/`judgeResolver()`. These need `acpSessionName` in their `completeOptions` to target the implementer session. `complete()` already supports `sessionName` option.
5. **Feature name threading:** Requires adding `featureName` param through 4 layers: review.ts ã orchestrator.ts ã runner.ts ã semantic.ts. Each layer is a simple optional param addition.

userStories.title: Normalize implementer session role in execution stage
userStories.description: Add `sessionRole: "implementer"` and conditional `keepSessionOpen: true` to the single-session `agent.run()` call in `execution.ts` so that no-test and test-after strategies produce the same session name (`nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-implementer`) as TDD strategies. Gate `keepSessionOpen` on review or rectification being enabled to avoid dangling sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: agent.run() in execution.ts (line 251) passes `sessionRole: "implementer"` for all test strategies it handles (no-test, test-after)
When `ctx.config.review.enabled` is true, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: true` to agent.run()
When `ctx.config.execution.rectification?.enabled` is true, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: true` to agent.run()
When both `ctx.config.review.enabled` and `ctx.config.execution.rectification?.enabled` are falsy, execution.ts passes `keepSessionOpen: false` to agent.run()

buildSessionName(storyWorkdir, ctx.prd.feature, ctx.story.id, "implementer") produces the same name as buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "implementer") in autofix.ts:301 for identical inputs

routing.reasoning: Single-file change adding two properties to an existing agent.run() call. ~10 LOC. Follows established pattern from session-runner.ts:171.

userStories.title: Thread featureName through semantic review call chain and fix debate path bug
userStories.description: Add `featureName?: string` parameter through the review call chain: review.ts ã orchestrator.ts ã runner.ts ã semantic.ts. Replace the hardcoded `nax-semantic-${story.id}` session name with `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "semantic")`. Fix the `featureName: story.id` bug in the debate branch at semantic.ts:392 where story ID is incorrectly used as the feature name.
acceptanceCriteria: runSemanticReview() signature includes `featureName?: string` as a parameter
runReview() in runner.ts signature includes `featureName?: string` and forwards it to runSemanticReview()
ReviewOrchestrator.review() in orchestrator.ts signature includes `featureName?: string` and forwards it to runReview()
review.ts stage passes `ctx.prd.feature` as `featureName` to reviewOrchestrator.review()
The complete() call in semantic.ts uses `buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, story.id, "semantic")` instead of the hardcoded `nax-semantic-${story.id}`
The debate branch at semantic.ts:392 passes the received `featureName` parameter instead of `story.id` as featureName to createDebateSession()
When featureName is undefined, buildSessionName() is still called and a debug log is emitted: "featureName missing ã semantic session name will not include feature"
The literal string `nax-semantic-${story.id}` no longer appears anywhere in the codebase

routing.reasoning: Touches 4 files threading a new parameter through the call chain. ~80 LOC across files. Includes a bug fix for the debate featureName. Medium complexity due to multi-file coordination.

userStories.title: Semantic review resumes implementer session via agent.run()
userStories.description: Replace `agent.complete()` in the non-debate path of semantic review with `agent.run()` that targets the implementer session by name using `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")`. The reviewer inherits the implementer's full session context. When the session is not found, ensureAcpSession() creates a new one (fail-open). The debate path (DebateSession) is not modified ã covered by US-004.
acceptanceCriteria: runSemanticReview() calls agent.run() instead of agent.complete() for the non-debate code path
agent.run() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` targeting the implementer session
agent.run() passes `keepSessionOpen: false` since semantic review is the last consumer
runSemanticReview() receives `workdir` (already present) and `featureName` (from US-002) and uses both in buildSessionName()
When agent.run() creates a new session (implementer session not found), a debug log is emitted: "implementer session not found ã semantic review running in new session"
The response extraction from agent.run() result correctly populates `rawResponse` ã handles AgentRunResult.output instead of CompleteResult
ReviewCheckResult shape returned by runSemanticReview() is unchanged ã callers receive the same interface
The debate path (reviewDebateEnabled branch at semantic.ts:382) is not modified by this story

routing.reasoning: Replaces complete() with run() in semantic.ts ã changes method call, session targeting, and response extraction. ~60 LOC. Follows established pattern from rectification-gate.ts. Medium due to behavioral change in how semantic review operates.

userStories.title: Debate resolver resumes implementer session
userStories.description: Extend `resolveOutcome()` in session-helpers.ts to accept `workdir` and `featureName` so the synthesis and custom resolvers can target the implementer session via `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")`. Thread `workdir` and `featureName` from StatefulCtx through the call site in session-stateful.ts. The majority resolver performs no LLM call and is unaffected. Individual debaters continue operating in their own per-debater sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: resolveOutcome() signature adds `workdir?: string` and `featureName?: string` parameters
synthesisResolver() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` in completeOptions when workdir is defined
judgeResolver() receives `acpSessionName: buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, "implementer")` in completeOptions when workdir is defined
The call site in session-stateful.ts passes `ctx.workdir` and `ctx.featureName` to resolveOutcome()

When resolveOutcome() is called with workdir defined but resolver type is majority-fail-closed or majority-fail-open, a warn log is emitted: "majority resolver does not support implementer session resumption ã switch to synthesis or custom resolver for context-aware semantic review"
majority resolver behavior is unchanged ã it returns vote count with resolverCostUsd: 0
When workdir is undefined, acpSessionName is not passed to synthesisResolver() or judgeResolver() (omitted from completeOptions)
DebateResult shape is unchanged ã callers receive the same interface

routing.reasoning: Touches 2 files (session-helpers.ts, session-stateful.ts) adding session targeting to resolver calls. ~50 LOC. Follows same buildSessionName pattern. Medium due to conditional logic around resolver types and warn logging for majority.
